rows.Title: Bixby not opening
rows.Problem: Bixby does not open properly when commanded; after receiving 'hi bixby', it opens briefly and then closes itself. It fails to open any app via Bixby command after closing the app and re-issuing the command.
rows.Ai Summary: Bixby fails to open properly and closes immediately after being commanded, preventing users from using its voice-activated features.
rows.Severity Reason: Users cannot access Bixby functionality, which is a core feature for device interaction, making the device unusable for basic tasks.

rows.Title: Bixby microphone not working
rows.Problem: Microphone becomes unresponsive and stops working when tapped; expected to work properly
rows.Ai Summary: Bixby's microphone fails to respond when tapped, preventing voice input and rendering the feature non-functional.
rows.Severity Reason: Users cannot use Bixby voice input, which is a core functionality, making the feature completely unusable.

rows.Title: Bixby voice commands setup
rows.Problem: When trying to set up voice commands for Bixby, it shows a pop-up suggesting a quieter environment even when the environment is quiet.
rows.Ai Summary: Bixby incorrectly prompts users to use a quieter environment when setting up voice commands, despite the environment being quiet.
rows.Severity Reason: The feature fails to recognize ambient noise conditions correctly, leading to an incorrect user prompt, but does not cause device failure or data loss.

rows.Title: No ringtone heard and no voice coming when call was connected
rows.Problem: No voice was heard from the earpiece during an incoming call after call pick up.
rows.Ai Summary: Users do not hear any sound during an incoming call, including no ringtone or voice from the earpiece.
rows.Severity Reason: The absence of audio during calls makes the device unusable for basic communication functions.

rows.Severity Reason: The application crashes, making it unusable for users who rely on browsing functions.

rows.Title: Different Text color appear on Clock widget

rows.Ai Summary: The clock widget on the home screen displays incorrect text color, which resolves when the widget is clicked.
rows.Severity Reason: The issue affects visual consistency of a functional widget but resolves upon interaction, indicating partial malfunction.

rows.Title: Samsung Keyboard - Keyboard engine otp suggestion not working sometimes
rows.Problem: Otp autofill does not work sometimes in apps like airtel thanks when entering mobile number for sim upgrade. OTP is detected in notification but keyboard engine fails to recognise or allow pasting the OTP.
rows.Ai Summary: The Samsung keyboard fails to recognise and allow pasting of OTPs in certain app scenarios, despite successfully detecting the OTP notification.
rows.Severity Reason: The feature fails intermittently in specific use cases, affecting user experience but not rendering the device unusable or causing data loss.

rows.Title: Google Discover (Home -1) page, swipe in landscape sluggish

rows.Title: Bixby UI Sluggish

rows.Title: Pasting text copied from Bixby to Samsung notes is invisible in dark mode

rows.Title: UI Overlap: "Cancel" and "Add" Buttons Obscure Text Entry in State Input Field
rows.Problem: When typing a state name into the input field, the "Cancel" and "Add" action buttons overlap the text entry area. The buttons are positioned directly over the input line, making it impossible for the user to see or verify the text they are currently typing. This layout conflict hinders data accuracy and usability. The "Cancel" and "Add" buttons should be positioned below or away from the input field. The text entry area must remain fully visible and unobstructed while the keyboard is active and the user is typing.
rows.Sub-Module: Galaxy AI > Now Nudge > PDI > Manage Personal Data > Address
rows.Ai Summary: The "Cancel" and "Add" buttons overlap the text input field in the state entry section of Galaxy AI's personal data management, obstructing user visibility and data accuracy.
rows.Severity Reason: Users cannot see or verify the text they are typing, which directly impacts data accuracy and usability, making basic input functions unreliable.

rows.Title: UI Mismatch: State Name Input Field Labeled as "Country" in PDI Settings
rows.Problem: Within the Address management section, the input field designated for entering a state name incorrectly displays "Country" as its header or title. The field title should accurately reflect the required input. If the field is intended for state/province information, the title should be updated to "State" or "Province" to align with the input instructions.
rows.Sub-Module: Galaxy AI > Now Nudge > PDI > Manage Personal Data > Address
rows.Ai Summary: The state name input field in the PDI settings incorrectly labels itself as 'Country' instead of 'State' or 'Province', causing confusion in user input.
rows.Severity Reason: The issue affects user input accuracy but does not prevent device functionality or cause crashes; it is a partial malfunction in UI labeling.

rows.Ai Summary: The Now Brief feature is not displaying on the home screen, although the widget exists and can be accessed via long press.
rows.Severity Reason: The feature is missing from the primary view but is accessible through a secondary action, indicating partial malfunction.

rows.Title: Link to Windows dont show anything if device rotated
rows.Problem: Link to Windows Initial setup -> Rotate device -> shown blank -> only monitor icon is seen -> rotate again -> takes time to load. No scrolling on this page is working.
rows.Ai Summary: When a device is rotated, the Link to Windows setup page shows a blank screen with only a monitor icon and does not allow scrolling.
rows.Severity Reason: Users cannot interact with the setup page after rotation, preventing completion of the initial configuration, which is a critical usability step.

rows.Title: no significant difference seen in charging time when fast charging is enabled
rows.Problem: When fast charging is enabled, the charging time decreases by 20 minutes, sometimes to 40-45 minutes, but the reduction is not consistent and does not show a significant difference in actual charging time compared to normal charging.
rows.Ai Summary: Fast charging results in inconsistent and non-significant reduction in charging time, indicating a performance issue in the charging system.
rows.Severity Reason: The charging time reduction is inconsistent and not substantial, leading to a partial malfunction in the fast charging feature.

rows.Title: no prompt to enable fast charging when fast charger is inserted
rows.Problem: When fast charging is off on S22, a popup prompts to enable fast charging. On Miracle, no such popup is shown.
rows.Ai Summary: Miracle device does not show a prompt to enable fast charging when a fast charger is inserted, unlike the S22.
rows.Severity Reason: The feature is missing in a key usage scenario, affecting user experience but does not cause device malfunction or data loss.

rows.Title: Settings / Privacy content still visible with custom privacy settings
rows.Problem: Notification panel privacy display shows content when custom privacy settings are applied, allowing visibility at angles over 150 degrees. This violates privacy and should be fixed.
rows.Ai Summary: Custom privacy settings in the notification panel fail to hide content, allowing visibility at high angles despite being set to maximum privacy.
rows.Severity Reason: Users can see sensitive content despite privacy settings, compromising data security and privacy expectations.

rows.Title: shows more than 90 mins to full charge with UE on 38% charge
rows.Problem: Shows 2hr 14 mins to fully charge from 13% when using a 65W charger with UE on. Same charger charges S22 from 14% in 1h 13 mins.
rows.Ai Summary: The device shows an unusually long charging time of 2 hours 14 minutes to reach 100% from 13% when using a 65W charger with UE enabled, while a similar charger charges another device in 1 hour 13 minutes.
rows.Severity Reason: The charging time is inconsistent with other devices and appears to be a performance deviation, but does not render the device unusable or cause data loss.

rows.Title: Slow Charging with Car Wireless Charger
rows.Problem: Slow charging when using a car wireless charger with an off-brand charging pad and cable, while the device cover is on.
rows.Ai Summary: The device charges very slowly when using a non-official car wireless charger with an off-brand pad and cable while the device cover is on.
rows.Severity Reason: The charging speed is reduced, but the device remains functional and does not crash or become unusable.

rows.Title: SamsungLocationSDK ALWAYS_RUNNING_ALLOWLIST
rows.Ai Summary: A location service has an always-running allowlist configuration that may pose security risks due to uncontrolled background activity.
rows.Severity Reason: The configuration allows continuous background operation of a location service without clear user consent, which could lead to privacy concerns, but does not immediately prevent device operation or cause crashes.

rows.Title: Slow charging using C brand charger
rows.Problem: Connecting device to C brand charger results in normal charging mode with no fast charging support
rows.Ai Summary: Device does not support fast charging when connected to a C brand charger, only charging at normal speed.
rows.Severity Reason: The device fails to utilize fast charging, which is a partial malfunction of charging performance, but does not render the device unusable.

rows.Title: Bixby can't tell recipe details
rows.Problem: Bixby shows recipe cards but errors when tapping to view details, preventing access to ingredients and full recipe information.
rows.Ai Summary: Bixby displays recipe cards but fails to show recipe details when tapped, preventing users from viewing ingredients or full recipe information.
rows.Severity Reason: Users cannot access essential recipe details, which is a core functionality of the Bixby recipe feature, making it unusable for basic use cases.

rows.Severity Reason: The error prevents certain actions from being performed during search, but the core functionality of searching remains partially available.

rows.Title: 10W charger showing Fast wireless Charging, whereas 15W rated charger is showing normal wireless charging

The device is charged with a Samsung 45W adapter and genuine Samsung cable, used with a UTT cover in horizontal position on a standing pad.
